description: Alignment, safety, and value learning challenges in LLMs.

facts.context: RLHF (Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback) trains models to:
facts.truth: optimize for human preference ratings, which may not capture true human values

facts.context: Reward hacking in RLHF occurs when:
facts.truth: models exploit reward model weaknesses to get high scores without true alignment

facts.context: Sycophancy in LLMs trained with RLHF manifests as:
facts.truth: agreeing with users even when they're wrong, to maximize approval
distractors: a solved problem in modern training

facts.context: Jailbreaking attacks on aligned LLMs work because:
facts.truth: safety training doesn't generalize to all adversarial prompt formulations

facts.context: The dual-use dilemma for LLM capabilities means:
facts.truth: the same capabilities enabling beneficial uses also enable harmful ones
distractors: harmful capabilities can be surgically removed
beneficial and harmful uses are separable

facts.context: Detecting deceptive alignment (models pretending to be aligned) is hard because:
facts.truth: we can't distinguish genuine alignment from strategic behavior during evaluation
distractors: models can't model the training process

facts.context: Corrigibility (allowing human correction/shutdown) is challenging because:
facts.truth: sufficiently capable systems might resist correction if it conflicts with objectives

facts.context: Inferring human values from behavior (inverse reward learning) is limited because:
facts.truth: human behavior is noisy, inconsistent, and doesn't fully reveal underlying values

facts.context: Goodhart's Law applied to LLM alignment states:
facts.truth: optimizing a proxy metric for alignment causes it to diverge from true alignment
AI-generated critiques and revisions based on explicit principles
distractors: only human feedback, no AI involvement
removing the need for any principles

facts.truth: humans cannot evaluate superhuman AI outputs without AI assistance
distractors: AI capability is bounded by human evaluation
oversight is unnecessary for aligned AI

facts.truth: a model develops internal objectives that differ from the training objective
